title: centaurea pratensis subsp. serotina
intro: Centaurea pratensis subsp. serotina, also known as Late Knawel, is a plant species within the daisy family, characterized by its small yellow flowers and feathery foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, as it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Centaurea pratensis subsp. serotina are said to include relief from stomach cramps, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal problems, as well as its potential to soothe respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for consumption, although its usage and efficacy may vary depending on the preparation method and individual tolerance.
